track.title: NSE Stocks for Beginners
track.description: A starter route for shares, dividends, CDS accounts, brokers, diversification, and market risk.

course.title: NSE Stocks for Beginners: Core route
course.description: Understand the listed-shares route before following tips or opening accounts.

lessons.title: What is a share?
lessons.summary: A share is ownership in a company, with price movement and business risk.
lessons.introduction: A share represents ownership in a company. Listed shares can be bought and sold through market routes, but prices can rise or fall and dividends are not automatic.
sections: Shares suit money that can handle price movement and time.
Company performance, market mood, costs, and news can affect price.
A beginner should learn the route before acting on tips.

scenario.title: Hot tip
scenario.text: A friend says a share will move quickly. The user checks company basics, broker route, fees, and whether this money can stay invested.

lessons.mistake: Using emergency money for a volatile asset.
lessons.action: Write why the money can or cannot handle price movement.
lessons.takeaway: Owning a share means accepting business and market uncertainty.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify market education and participant information using NSE and CMA sources.

lessons.title: Dividends and capital gains
lessons.summary: Share returns may come from dividends or price change, but neither is assured.
lessons.introduction: A listed share can create value through dividends, price movement, or both, but outcomes can also disappoint. Tax treatment should be checked with official sources or a professional.
sections: A dividend depends on company performance and decisions.
A capital gain or loss depends on selling price compared with buying cost.
Fees and taxes can affect the result and should not be guessed.

scenario.title: Dividend expectation
scenario.text: A user buys because last year's dividend was discussed online. They check whether future dividends can change.

lessons.mistake: Assuming a past dividend or past price movement repeats.
lessons.action: Separate dividend expectation from price-change expectation in your notes.
lessons.takeaway: Shares need patience, research, and risk tolerance.
lessons.source_note: Source note: KRA links are tax education references only; PesaRoute does not give tax advice.

lessons.title: CDS account and broker basics
lessons.summary: Understand the account and intermediary route before trading listed shares.
lessons.introduction: Buying listed shares usually involves a CDS account and a licensed or approved market participant. The route matters because it affects custody, records, fees, and support.
sections: A CDS account is an electronic securities account in the central depository system.
A trading participant or central depository agent helps with account and trading access.
Verify the participant independently before sending documents or money.

scenario.title: Account opening
scenario.text: A user compares two brokers and checks NSE/CDSC/CMA sources before submitting documents.

lessons.mistake: Opening an account through an unverifiable contact because the process seems faster.
lessons.action: List the official source you used to verify the broker or account route.
lessons.takeaway: Account route verification is part of investor protection.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify CDS and participant details through CDSC, NSE, and CMA public sources.

lessons.title: Journal prompt: Why this share route?
lessons.summary: Write the thesis, risk, time horizon, and exit reason before buying externally.
lessons.introduction: A share decision note should record why the route fits the goal, what can go wrong, and how long the money can stay invested. This reduces tip-driven action.
sections: Use an amount range if exact values feel private.
Record what would make you wait.
Do not confuse learning notes with an instruction to buy.

scenario.title: Private note
scenario.text: A user writes that this is long-term learning money, not rent or emergency money.

lessons.mistake: Buying first and writing the reason later.
lessons.action: Save a journal entry with thesis, risk, timeline, and source checked.
lessons.takeaway: A written reason helps you notice when a decision is only social pressure.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify provider and market information externally before any action.

quiz_questions.prompt: What is a CDS account used for?
quiz_questions.explanation: CDS accounts support electronic securities records.

quiz_questions.prompt: Why are shares not ideal for emergency money?
options: Price can move and liquidity depends on the market
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Price can move and liquidity depends on the market

flashcards.example: A listed company share trades through market routes.

flashcards.back: A company distribution to shareholders when declared.
flashcards.example: It can change year to year.

flashcards.back: A gain when selling above cost, before considering fees or tax.
The central depository organization for Kenyan securities records.
To reduce fraud and account-route risk.
